title: Go security parity boundary
go_runtime_scope: local/copied-DB-and-data security fixtures and runtime guards

security_controls.public_bind: Non-local listen addresses are refused unless PRISM_GO_ALLOW_PUBLIC_BIND=1 is explicitly set.
security_controls.auth: Go runtime declares no built-in auth/token layer in /healthz and docs.
security_controls.exposure_policy: Trusted LAN/VPN/proxy-auth only; do not expose Prism directly to the public internet.
security_controls.path_traversal: Attachment/upload/static file paths stay confined to PRISM_GO_DATA_DIR roots.
security_controls.ssrf: Upload URL rejects private, loopback, link-local, multicast, unspecified, reserved, and redirected unsafe targets.
security_controls.mime_and_size: Uploads validate extension, magic/MIME, image decode, and 5 MiB cap before writes.

no_mutation_failures: private/loopback upload-url requests leave static/uploads unchanged
bad MIME multipart upload requests leave static/uploads unchanged
static traversal requests do not read adjacent files
non-local bind failure exits before serving requests

evidence: /healthz reports auth=none and public bind policy
SSRF and bad MIME fixtures return 400 without DB/file mutation

next_recommended_task.reason: Security boundary is locked locally; package smoke should prove the same runtime contract from a fresh Windows artifact.
